item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran FM says trust deficit main obstacle in US negotiations
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Tehran cannot trust the US and would only negotiate if Washington shows seriousness, during a press conference in New Delhi following a BRICS meeting.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran doubts US seriousness about nuclear talks amid deadlock
item.description: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi expressed distrust of US intentions regarding war negotiations, saying enriched uranium remains a point of deadlock in any potential talks.

item.title: 🌍 BRICS talks end without joint statement over Iran war divisions
item.description: A BRICS meeting in India failed to produce a joint statement as members could not agree on language regarding the Iran conflict, exposing diplomatic rifts within the bloc.

item.title: 🇰🇷 Samsung faces 18-day strike as AI boom sparks worker divisions
item.description: South Korean chip giant Samsung faces an impending 18-day strike that has rattled investors and threatens global supply chains, driven by disputes over how AI boom profits should be shared with workers.

item.title: ⚔️ Live updates: Iran says cannot trust Americans as Trump claims Strait control
item.description: Iran stated it cannot trust the US at all in negotiations, while President Trump claimed the US controls the Strait of Hormuz and called for Iran to reach a deal amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian Missile Strike Kills 24 in Kyiv Apartment Building
item.description: A Russian missile struck a residential building in Kyiv, killing 24 people including three children. President Zelenskiy called for punishment of Moscow as rescue workers recovered bodies from the rubble.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Arrests Ukrainian National Suspected of Spying for Russia
item.description: German prosecutors announced the arrest of a Ukrainian national on suspicion of espionage for Russia. The case highlights ongoing intelligence threats in Europe amid the Ukraine war.

item.title: ⚔️ US Admiral: Iran Military Capability Dramatically Degraded
item.description: The US admiral commanding the Iran war effort said American strikes have left Tehran with only small or moderate ability to strike its neighbors, describing major damage to Irans defense industry.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran FM: No Trust in US, Will Negotiate Only If Serious
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said Tehran has no trust in Washington and will only negotiate if the US shows seriousness. He added that all vessels can pass through Hormuz except those at war with Iran.

item.title: 🇵🇱 US Cancels Planned Deployment of 4,000 Troops to Poland
item.description: The Pentagon scrapped plans to temporarily deploy 4,000 US-based troops to Poland, raising fresh questions about Trumps expected troop cuts in Europe and NATOs eastern flank security.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran says Pakistan mediation with US at impasse over uranium enrichment
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Pakistan-mediated talks with the US face deadlock over the enriched uranium issue, while welcoming potential Chinese diplomatic involvement.

item.title: 🌍 UN agencies warn Somalia faces renewed famine risk as hunger crisis deepens
item.description: Nearly 6 million people face acute food insecurity in Somalia with famine risk emerging in the southwest, as UN agencies call for urgent international assistance.

item.title: 🚁 Ukraine races to deploy AI-powered drone swarms to counter Russian manpower advantage
item.description: Ukrainian defense industry is advancing swarm drone technology that would allow few operators to deploy dozens or hundreds of autonomous attack craft, though mass battlefield deployment remains years away.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Economy Expected to Grow for Second Quarter Despite Iran War Shadow
item.description: Japan's GDP likely rose in January-March on recovering exports and solid domestic demand, a Reuters poll showed, though the full impact of the Iran conflict on the global economy has yet to be felt.

item.title: 🛢️ Gazprom Astrakhan Gas Plant Halts Fuel Output After Drone Attack Fire
item.description: Gazprom's gas processing plant in Russia's southern Astrakhan region has halted motor fuel production after a fire broke out following a drone attack on May 13, two industry sources told Reuters.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iraqi Parliament Approves Partial Government Under New PM Zaidi
item.description: Iraq's new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi was sworn in with only a partial cabinet after lawmakers failed to reach consensus on key postings including interior and defense, amid political uncertainty in a country heavily affected by the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇧🇴 Bolivian Miners Clash With Police in La Paz, Demand President Resign
item.description: Violent clashes erupted in La Paz as mining groups took to the streets calling for the resignation of centrist President Rodrigo Paz, with explosions heard during the protests in Bolivia's capital.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Trump Reaffirms Ironclad US-Japan Alliance After China Summit
item.description: Japanese PM Takaichi confirmed that Trump reaffirmed the ironclad bilateral alliance in a phone call after his Beijing visit, signaling continued US commitment to Asian allies despite Trump-Xi talks.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Patience With Iran Running Out
item.description: President Trump stated he would not be "much more patient" with Iran, urging Tehran to reach a deal while suggesting the quest for enriched uranium was more about perception than security.

item.title: ⛽ Ukraine Doubles Strikes on Russian Oil Refineries
item.description: Ukraine has stepped up drone attacks on Russian energy infrastructure, doubling the number of oil refineries targeted since the start of the year, according to Russian officials on social media.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Trade Deficit Widens as Mideast War Drives Oil Imports
item.description: India merchandise trade deficit expanded to $28.38 billion in April as crude imports surged to a six-month high, with Middle East conflict disrupting supplies and raising oil prices.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump Discusses Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
item.description: President Trump said he discussed lifting sanctions on Chinese companies that purchase Iranian oil with Xi Jinping and will make a decision in the coming days, signaling potential policy shift.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ Israel Adopts Rubble Doctrine in Southern Lebanon Security Policy
item.description: A profound shift in Israeli military thinking after October 7 has led to a new security doctrine focused on denying enemy positions in southern Lebanon through systematic destruction, as seen in El-Khiam.

item.title: 🇦🇺🛢️ Australia Rules Out LNG Export Controls as Gas Supply Fears Ease
item.description: Australia will not impose LNG export controls for Q3 2026 after industry assurances eased concerns about east coast gas supply, maintaining its position as a key global LNG supplier.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 AI and Semiconductors Loom Over Trump-Xi Summit Despite Trade Focus
item.description: While trade dominated the Trump-Xi talks, artificial intelligence and semiconductor technology remained an undercurrent in the discussions, highlighting the tech competition between the superpowers.
